cards.back: A morphological operation that removes small details from an image, effectively reducing its noise and improving its contrast.
A morphological operation that increases the size of an image by adding pixels to its boundaries, often used for edge detection and smoothing.
A morphological operation that combines erosion and dilation to remove small details and fill holes in an image, while preserving large features.
A morphological operation that combines dilation and erosion to remove small details and fill holes in an image, while preserving large features and edges.
In the context of morphological image processing, a hole refers to a small region in an image that contains no pixels, often resulting from noise or missing data.
